response.justification: While the candidate has a broad 15-plus-year engineering career and recent exposure to AI-agent tooling, the role demands recognized, deep AI expertise and director-level strategic advisory capability. The AI experience is confined to roughly one year in the current role, there is no demonstrated track record advising organizations on AI strategy or building production AI products at scale, and the background is fundamentally that of a skilled full-stack and systems engineer rather than an AI-focused technical leader.
strengths: Broad, polyglot engineering background spanning 15+ years across web, embedded, infrastructure, and systems
Entrepreneurial track record with GISCollective and multiple open-source projects showing builder instincts
Recent hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks shows awareness of the current AI tooling landscape
concerns: AI expertise is shallow and almost entirely limited to the current role started in April 2025 — no demonstrated products or platforms built with AI at meaningful scale
No evidence of director-level strategic advisory experience: customer-facing thought leadership, executive engagement, or cross-org AI strategy work is absent from the resume
No advanced degree in AI or ML; education is a BS in CS and a fine-arts bachelor's, with no specialization aligned to the role's preferred qualifications

key_factors.factor: Lack of AI depth and track record
No strategic or advisory experience at required seniority
Broad engineering tenure and entrepreneurial experience
